lines.text: Up to now we've been working with toy games, stripped-down scenarios just complex enough to see the underlying forces clearly.
Those forces have names you know by now: polarization, indifference, balance, and reciprocal ranges.
Real No-Limit Hold'em looks nothing like a toy game on the surface, but underneath, the same forces are pulling the strings.
The big differences are volume and complexity: you have many streets, enormous starting ranges, and hundreds of possible board textures.
That scale means you cannot hold the full solution in your head, but you don't need to.
What you need is to recognize which force is dominant in any given spot, and let that guide your instincts.
So in this chapter we take everything you've built and plug it into a real hand, one street at a time.
By the end you'll have a framework for reading any spot and asking the right questions, even ones this series never covered.
